meta.title: PicoMUTX — Build and Deploy AI Agents Without Hiring a Developer
meta.description: PicoMUTX gives founders and operators one practical path from AI-agent idea to trusted launch, with implementation guidance, safeguards, and real support built in.

hero.title: Build and deploy AI agents
hero.subtitle: PicoMUTX takes you from AI-agent idea to a working, reviewable system with one guided build path, grounded support, and guardrails before launch.
hero.meta: Free to pre-register · No credit card required · Human reply, not a nurture funnel

items.0: Built for founders, operators, and lean teams
items.1: One path from first use case to live agent
items.2: Support, safeguards, and escalation in one loop

problem.title: The idea is rarely
problem.body: Most AI-agent projects stall after the first real decision: what to use, what to trust, what to ship first, and who can help when the path stops being obvious.

0.label: "I know the use case. I do not know the stack."
0.body: Every guide assumes a different toolset, a different skill level, and a different definition of done. The distance from concept to first working agent stays larger than it should.

1.label: "I already got halfway and lost momentum."
1.body: The first blocker cost more than time. It killed confidence, and nobody around you could tell you the exact next move with enough certainty.

2.label: "I already paid for help and still do not trust the result."
2.body: Now the problem is not just delivery. It is whether the system is safe, legible, and worth putting in front of real work.

3.label: "I do not want to launch something I cannot explain."
3.body: If you cannot see what it costs, why it failed, or how it behaves under pressure, you do not have an asset yet.

problem.close: PicoMUTX exists to close that gap.

platform.title: One guided system from first build to trusted launch.
platform.body: PicoMUTX is not another folder of tutorials. It gives you the build path, the support layer, and the operating safeguards in the same product, so progress does not disappear the moment something breaks.

0.title: Paths built around the job to be done
0.body: Start from the outcome you want, then follow a sequence designed to get one useful agent working instead of collecting disconnected theory.

1.title: Help that knows where you are stuck
1.body: When progress breaks, PicoMUTX responds from the same path and context instead of pushing you back into generic docs.

2.title: Guardrails before the first real launch
2.body: Track cost, behavior, and approval boundaries before the agent touches work you actually care about.

3.title: Expert escalation when the edge case is real
3.body: Static content takes you far. When nuance matters, you have a faster route to a human decision.

who.title: PicoMUTX is not for everyone.
who.notForYouTitle: This is not for you if…

forYou.0: You want to build an AI agent but don't want to depend entirely on developers
forYou.1: You've tried before and got stuck halfway
forYou.2: You already spent money on tools or implementation and still don't trust the outcome
forYou.3: You want a practical path, not more theory
forYou.4: You want support while building, not only after something goes wrong

notForYou.0: You are looking for a shortcut with zero learning
notForYou.1: You already run a mature enterprise agent stack and need complex governance infrastructure
notForYou.2: You want entertainment, trends, or hype more than execution
notForYou.3: You are not ready to change how the business actually operates
notForYou.4: You expect AI to replace judgment, leadership, or accountability

beforeAfter.title: What changes when the build path stops fracturing

0.before: You have a use case, but no reliable next move
0.after: You have a visible sequence and a named next step

1.before: You jump between tools, tutorials, and half-relevant advice
1.after: You build, review, and troubleshoot in one surface

2.before: One blocker drains momentum and confidence
2.after: One blocker becomes the next action instead of the end

3.before: Your prototype stays risky, vague, or hidden
3.after: Your agent becomes something you can review, trust, and run

beforeAfter.close: The goal is not to admire AI. The goal is to ship one useful agent you can understand, trust, and improve.

earlyAccess.title: Founding access is open now. It will not stay that way.
earlyAccess.body: Pre-register now to get into the first release wave while the product is still being shaped with early operators. That means earlier onboarding, tighter feedback loops, and a clearer line into what gets built next.

benefits.0: Get in before the public opening
benefits.3: Influence over early workflows and use cases
benefits.4: First visibility into final pricing and access terms

enterprise.name: Enterprise
enterprise.description: For teams rolling this out across the business

faq.title: Questions worth clearing up before you commit

0.q: Do I need to know how to code?
0.a: No. PicoMUTX is for people who need the outcome without turning themselves into full-time agent engineers.

1.q: What if I've already tried and failed?
1.a: That is one of the main use cases. PicoMUTX is built to recover momentum after the first confusing, expensive, or credibility-killing blocker.

2.q: What kind of agents can I build?
2.a: Practical ones: support agents, internal copilots, workflow automations, and business systems that remove real manual work. The focus is operational usefulness, not toy demos.

3.a: Yes. Pre-registration is free and requires no payment. You join the first release queue and hear first when founding access opens.

4.a: PicoMUTX is strongest for founders, operators, and smaller teams that need a clear route to something working. Heavier enterprise governance belongs in the broader MUTX stack.

finalCta.title: Stop leaving the agent in planning mode.
finalCta.body: If the use case matters, the next step is not another tab. It is a path that gets one working system into the open with less risk, less drift, and less wasted motion.
